definitions: To send or deliver (something) to a person's care or for a particular purpose.
To put (something) into a usually final and undesirable state or place.
To commit (someone or something) to a particular fate or destiny.
examples: She decided to consign the old furniture to the attic.
The artist will consign the painting to the gallery for exhibition.
He was consigned to a life of poverty.
The company will consign the goods for shipment overseas.
